title: Argentina USD/ARS Exchange Rates -- All Tiers, Daily (Official, Wholesale, Blue/Black-Market, CCL, MEP, Card/Tourist, Solidario, Crypto)
source_org: ArgentinaDatos (argentinadatos.com) -- an open public API for Argentine economic/financial data, itself aggregating from Ambito Financiero and other standard Argentine financial-media sources
retrieved_by: claude (direct curl, no key/auth required, single JSON GET)
time_coverage: 2011-01-03 to 2026-07-12 (oficial/mayorista/blue: full range, 5,670 obs each; contadoconliqui/CCL from 2013-01-02, 4,940 obs; bolsa/MEP from 2018-10-29, 2,814 obs; tarjeta/card-tourist from 2019-12-23, 2,394 obs; solidario/COVID-tax-rate 2020-01-02 to 2023-12-13 only -- discontinued when the tax was repealed, 1,442 obs; cripto from 2023-02-07, 1,252 obs)
license_terms: ArgentinaDatos public API -- freely accessible, no key required, no explicit license statement found on the site (treated as public financial-media-sourced data, standard for this project's FX-tracker sources e.g. TGJU/Bonbast for Iran)
notes: COMPARATOR FIND for the parallel-FX mission (Iran-first project, comparator opportunistically grabbed per mission brief -- Argentina explicitly named as a good direct comparison to Iran's black-market FX premium history). This is the DIRECT ARGENTINE EQUIVALENT of this project's existing data/raw/fx-parallel-rate/usd-irr-parallel-history/ (TGJU Iran parallel rate, 2011-2026) -- same date range start almost exactly (Argentina 2011-01-03 vs Iran TGJU 2011-11-26), same daily frequency, directly chartable side-by-side. Fills the confirmed gap noted in data/raw/bcra-argentina/bcra-monetary-fx/manifest.json, which explicitly flagged that BCRA's own official API does NOT publish an informal/parallel/blue rate (by design -- central banks only report their own regulated operations) and that an independent source would be needed. 'blue' = the informal/black-market cash rate (the direct analogue to Iran's TGJU free-market rate); 'oficial' = official CBI-equivalent rate (already covered for Argentina via bcra-argentina); 'mayorista' = wholesale reference rate; 'contadoconliqui' (CCL) and 'bolsa' (MEP) = the two main LEGAL-BUT-INDIRECT capital-account channels Argentines use to effectively buy dollars via securities transactions, priced close to but not identical to the blue rate; 'tarjeta' = the card/tourist rate (official rate plus tax surcharges, for foreign card purchases); 'solidario' = the pandemic-era 'Impuesto PAIS'-loaded rate for individual dollar-savings purchases, active 2020-2023 and correctly shows as discontinued (not a download failure) once that tax regime ended. Every rate type kept as found -- no fabrication, no gap-filling.
